description: Iron Mountain is an enterprise information management company that stores, governs, digitizes, destroys, hosts, and retires business information and physical assets, which is less glamorous than a productivity app and much harder to ignore when auditors arrive.

overview.title: Core Business
paragraphs: Iron Mountain is best known for records storage, records management, secure shredding, and information governance services for enterprises, governments, and regulated organizations. Its practical value proposition is custody: know where information is, control who can access it, retain it only as long as required, and destroy it without turning compliance into performance art.

overview.title: Beyond Records
paragraphs: The company has expanded from physical records and secure destruction into digital platforms, scanning and digitization, intelligent document processing, data protection, data centers, and IT asset lifecycle management. This makes Iron Mountain a hybrid infrastructure and services provider: part warehouse, part compliance vendor, part data-center landlord, and part enterprise workflow platform.

overview.title: Registry Relevance
paragraphs: For Free The World, Iron Mountain should be mapped across two different surfaces. The physical services side competes with local storage, shredding, logistics, and in-house records programs, while the digital side raises questions about proprietary platforms, data portability, custody, metadata access, cloud dependencies, and vendor lock-in.

overview.title: Initial Product Map
paragraphs: The known seeds, Records management and Secure shredding, are appropriate starting points. Likely follow-on products or product families include Iron Mountain Connect, InSight DXP, scanning and digitization, offsite tape vaulting, data centers and colocation, and Asset Lifecycle Management or ITAD services.
